title: Manufacturing Journey Assessment - Set 3
description: Assessment covering the complete stainless steel manufacturing process from Raw Material Procurement to Dispatch.

questions.question: Which alloying element gives stainless steel its corrosion resistance?
questions.explanation: Chromium forms a passive oxide layer that protects stainless steel from corrosion.

questions.question: Before charging scrap into the EAF, it is prepared in the:
questions.explanation: The Scrap Yard prepares and segregates scrap before melting.

questions.question: Which furnace performs the initial melting of scrap?
questions.explanation: The Electric Arc Furnace melts the scrap using electric arcs.

questions.question: Which process is mainly responsible for reducing carbon content in molten steel?
questions.explanation: The AOD process removes carbon while preserving chromium.

questions.question: Which unit adjusts the final chemical composition before casting?
questions.explanation: LRF performs secondary refining and composition adjustment.

questions.question: The output of the Continuous Casting Machine is:
questions.explanation: CCM continuously casts molten steel into slabs.

questions.question: Which rolling process is performed first?
questions.explanation: Hot Rolling is performed before Cold Rolling.

questions.question: Which process improves the dimensional accuracy of stainless steel?
questions.explanation: Cold Rolling provides better dimensional accuracy and surface finish.

questions.question: Annealing mainly restores:
questions.explanation: Annealing restores ductility and relieves internal stress.

questions.question: Which process prepares stainless steel for its final surface finish?
questions.explanation: Grinding removes imperfections and improves surface finish.

questions.question: The Electric Arc Furnace uses graphite electrodes for melting.
questions.explanation: Graphite electrodes generate electric arcs that melt the scrap.

questions.question: Cold Rolling increases dimensional accuracy.
questions.explanation: Cold Rolling improves both dimensional accuracy and surface finish.

questions.question: Warehouse operations begin before steel making.
questions.explanation: Warehouse activities mainly involve storing raw materials, semi-finished and finished products after manufacturing stages.

questions.question: Pickling removes oxide scale formed during heat treatment.
questions.explanation: Pickling uses acid treatment to remove oxide scale and restore a clean surface.

questions.question: Dispatch is responsible for transporting finished products to customers.
questions.explanation: Dispatch is the final stage where finished goods are prepared and shipped.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) in stainless steel manufacturing?
questions.sampleAnswer: The Electric Arc Furnace melts stainless steel scrap and alloy materials to produce molten steel for further refining.

questions.question: Why is the Ladle Refining Furnace (LRF) important?
questions.sampleAnswer: The LRF performs secondary refining, adjusts temperature, and achieves the required chemical composition.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Hot Rolling?
questions.sampleAnswer: Hot Rolling reduces slab thickness and converts slabs into hot rolled coils or plates.

questions.question: Why is Grinding performed before final inspection?
questions.sampleAnswer: Grinding removes surface defects and improves the surface finish before quality inspection.

questions.question: Mention any two documents commonly prepared during Dispatch.
questions.sampleAnswer: Examples include Invoice, Delivery Challan, Packing List, Quality Certificate, and E-Way Bill.

questions.question: Which process directly follows Cold Rolling?
questions.explanation: Annealing is carried out after Cold Rolling to restore ductility.

questions.question: Which department verifies the final product before it is released for dispatch?
questions.explanation: The Quality department performs the final inspection before dispatch.

questions.question: Which equipment is commonly used to move heavy coils inside the warehouse?
questions.explanation: Forklifts are commonly used for safe material handling inside warehouses.

questions.question: Which practice helps ensure accurate inventory management?
questions.explanation: FIFO (First In, First Out) helps maintain inventory accuracy and reduces material aging.

questions.question: Which manufacturing stage comes immediately before Dispatch?
questions.explanation: After quality inspection, finished products are stored in the Warehouse before being dispatched to customers.
